text.KJV: Chusing rather to suffer affliction with the people of God, then to enioy the pleasures of sinne for a season:
text.NABRE: he chose to be ill-treated along with the people of God rather than enjoy the fleeting pleasure of sin.

text.NRSV: He considered abuse suffered for the Christ to be greater wealth than the treasures of Egypt, for he was looking ahead to the reward.
text.Rotherham: Accounting—as greater riches than Egypt’s treasures, the reproach of the Anointed One; for he was looking away unto the recompense.
text.DRB: Esteeming the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasure of the Egyptians. For he looked unto the reward.
text.KJV: Esteeming the reproch of Christ greater riches then the treasures in Egypt: for he had respect vnto the recompense of the reward.
text.NABRE: He considered the reproach of the Anointed greater wealth than the treasures of Egypt, for he was looking to the recompense.

text.NRSV: By faith he left Egypt, unafraid of the king’s anger; for he persevered as though he saw him who is invisible.
text.Rotherham: By faith, he forsook Egypt—not put in fear of the wrath of the king; for, as seeing him who cannot be seen, he persevered.
text.DRB: By faith he left Egypt, not fearing the fierceness of the king: for he endured as seeing him that is invisible.
text.KJV: By faith hee forsooke Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king: for he indured, as seeing him who is inuisible.
text.NABRE: By faith he left Egypt, not fearing the king’s fury, for he persevered as if seeing the one who is invisible.

text.NRSV: By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood, so that the destroyer of the firstborn would not touch the firstborn of Israel.
text.Rotherham: By faith, he hath kept the passover and the besmearing of the blood, lest, he that was destroying the first-born, should be touching them.
text.DRB: By faith he celebrated the pasch, and the shedding of the blood; that he, who destroyed the firstborn, might not touch them.
text.KJV: Through faith he kept the Passeouer, and the sprinkling of blood, lest he that destroyed the first borne, should touch them.
text.NABRE: By faith he kept the Passover and sprinkled the blood, that the Destroyer of the firstborn might not touch them.

text.NRSV: By faith the people passed through the Red Sea as if it were dry land, but when the Egyptians attempted to do so they were drowned.
text.Rotherham: By faith, they passed through the Red Sea, as over dry land,—which the Egyptians, seizing an attempt to do, were swallowed up.
text.DRB: By faith they passed through the Red Sea, as by dry land: which the Egyptians attempting, were swallowed up.
text.KJV: By faith they passed through the red sea, as by drie land: which the Egyptians assaying to do, were drowned.
text.NABRE: By faith they crossed the Red Sea as if it were dry land, but when the Egyptians attempted it they were drowned.

text.NRSV: By faith the walls of Jericho fell after they had been encircled for seven days.
text.Rotherham: By faith, the walls of Jericho, fell, having been surrounded for seven days.
text.DRB: By faith the walls of Jericho fell down, by the going round them seven days.
text.KJV: By faith the walles of Iericho fell downe, after they were compassed about seuen dayes.
text.NABRE: By faith the walls of Jericho fell after being encircled for seven days.

text.NRSV: By faith Rahab the prostitute did not perish with those who were disobedient, because she had given a kindly welcome to the spies.
text.Rotherham: By faith, Rahab the harlot perished not with them who refused to yield, she having welcomed the spies with peace.
text.DRB: By faith Rahab the harlot perished not with the unbelievers, receiving the spies with peace.
text.KJV: By faith the harlot Rahab perished not with them that beleeued not, when shee had receiued the spies with peace.
text.NABRE: By faith Rahab the harlot did not perish with the disobedient, for she had received the spies in peace.

text.NRSV: And what more should I say? For time would fail me to tell of Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, of David and Samuel and the prophets—
text.Rotherham: And what more can I say? For, time, will fail me while I go on telling—concerning Gideon, Barak, Sampson, Jephthah, David also, and Samuel, and the prophets,—
text.DRB: And what shall I yet say? For the time would fail me to tell of Gedeon, Barac, Samson, Jephthe, David, Samuel, and the prophets:
text.KJV: And what shall I more say? for the time would faile mee to tell of Gideon, and of Barak, and of Sampson, and of Iephthah, of Dauid also and Samuel, and of the Prophets:
text.NABRE: What more shall I say? I have not time to tell of Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, of David and Samuel and the prophets,

text.NRSV: who through faith conquered kingdoms, administered justice, obtained promises, shut the mouths of lions,
text.Rotherham: Who, through faith—prevailed in contest over kingdoms, wrought righteousness, attained unto promises, shut the mouths of lions,
text.DRB: Who by faith conquered kingdoms, wrought justice, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions,
text.KJV: Who through faith subdued kingdomes, wrought righteousnesse, obteined promises, stopped the mouthes of Lions,
text.NABRE: who by faith conquered kingdoms, did what was righteous, obtained the promises; they closed the mouths of lions,

text.NRSV: quenched raging fire, escaped the edge of the sword, won strength out of weakness, became mighty in war, put foreign armies to flight.
text.Rotherham: Quenched the power of fire, escaped the mouths of the sword, were made powerful from weakness, became mighty in battle, overturned, camps of aliens;
text.DRB: Quenched the violence of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, recovered strength from weakness, became valiant in battle, put to flight the armies of foreigners:
text.KJV: Quenched the violence of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, out of weakenesse were made strong, waxed valiant in fight, turned to flight the armies of the aliens.
text.NABRE: put out raging fires, escaped the devouring sword; out of weakness they were made powerful, became strong in battle, and turned back foreign invaders.

text.NRSV: Women received their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured, refusing to accept release, in order to obtain a better resurrection.
text.Rotherham: Women received, by resurrection, their dead; but, others, were put to the rack, not accepting redemption, that, unto a better resurrection, they might attain:
text.DRB: Women received their dead raised to life again. But others were racked, not accepting deliverance, that they might find a better resurrection.
text.KJV: Women receiued their dead raised to life againe: and others were tortured, not accepting deliuerance, that they might obtaine a better resurrection.
text.NABRE: Women received back their dead through resurrection. Some were tortured and would not accept deliverance, in order to obtain a better resurrection.

text.NRSV: Others suffered mocking and flogging, and even chains and imprisonment.
text.Rotherham: Others, again, of mockings and scourgings, received trial, nay! further, of bonds and imprisonments:
text.DRB: And others had trial of mockeries and stripes, moreover also of bands and prisons.
text.KJV: And others had triall of cruell mockings and scourgings, yea moreouer, of bonds and imprisonment.
text.NABRE: Others endured mockery, scourging, even chains and imprisonment.

text.NRSV: They were stoned to death, they were sawn in two, they were killed by the sword; they went about in skins of sheep and goats, destitute, persecuted, tormented—
text.Rotherham: They were stoned, were pierced through, were sawn asunder, by murder, with a sword, died, went about in sheep-skins, in goat-hides,—being in want, suffering tribulation, enduring ill-treatment:
text.DRB: They were stoned, they were cut asunder, they were tempted, they were put to death by the sword, they wandered about in sheepskins, in goatskins, being in want, distressed, afflicted:
text.KJV: They were stoned, they were sawen asunder, were tempted, were slaine with the sword: they wandered about in sheepskinnes, and goat skins, being destitute, afflicted, tormented.
text.NABRE: They were stoned, sawed in two, put to death at sword’s point; they went about in skins of sheep or goats, needy, afflicted, tormented.

text.NRSV: of whom the world was not worthy. They wandered in deserts and mountains, and in caves and holes in the ground.
text.Rotherham: Of whom the world, was not worthy—upon deserts, wandering, and mountains, and in caves,—and in the caverns of the earth.
text.DRB: Of whom the world was not worthy; wandering in deserts, in mountains, and in dens, and in caves of the earth.
text.KJV: Of whome the world was not worthy: they wandered in deserts, and in mountains, and in dennes and caues of the earth.
text.NABRE: The world was not worthy of them. They wandered about in deserts and on mountains, in caves and in crevices in the earth.

text.NRSV: Yet all these, though they were commended for their faith, did not receive what was promised,
text.Rotherham: And, these all, though they obtained witness through their faith, yet bare not away, the promise:
text.DRB: And all these being approved by the testimony of faith, received not the promise;
text.KJV: And these all hauing obtained a good report through faith, receiued not the promise:
text.NABRE: Yet all these, though approved because of their faith, did not receive what had been promised.

text.NRSV: since God had provided something better so that they would not, apart from us, be made perfect.
text.Rotherham: God, for us, something better providing,—that, not apart from us, should they be made, perfect.
text.DRB: God providing some better thing for us, that they should not be perfected without us.
text.KJV: God hauing prouided some better thing for vs, that they without vs, should not be made perfect.
text.NABRE: God had foreseen something better for us, so that without us they should not be made perfect.

text.NRSV: Therefore, since we are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses, let us also lay aside every weight and the sin that clings so closely, and let us run with perseverance the race that is set before us,
text.Rotherham: Therefore, indeed, seeing that, we also, have encircling us, so great a cloud of witnesses, stripping off every incumbrance and the easily entangling sin, with endurance, let us be running, the race that is lying before us,
text.DRB: And therefore we also having so great a cloud of witnesses over our head, laying aside every weight and sin which surrounds us, let us run by patience to the fight proposed to us:
text.KJV: Wherefore, seeing wee also are compassed about with so great a cloude of witnesses, let vs lay aside euery weight, & the sinne which doth so easily beset vs, and let vs runne with patience vnto the race that is set before vs,
text.NABRE: Chapter 12 God Our Father. Therefore, since we are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses, let us rid ourselves of every burden and sin that clings to us and persevere in running the race that lies before us

text.NRSV: looking to Jesus the pioneer and perfecter of our faith, who for the sake of the joy that was set before him endured the cross, disregarding its shame, and has taken his seat at the right hand of the throne of God.
text.Rotherham: Looking away unto our faith’s Princely-leader and perfecter, Jesus,—who, in consideration of the joy lying before him, endured a cross, shame, despising! And, on the right hand of the throne of God, hath taken his seat.
text.DRB: Looking on Jesus, the author and finisher of faith, who having joy set before him, endured the cross, despising the shame, and now sitteth on the right hand of the throne of God.
text.KJV: Looking vnto Iesus the Authour and finisher of our faith, who for the ioy that was set before him, endured the crosse, despising the shame, and is set down at the right hand of the throne of God.
text.NABRE: while keeping our eyes fixed on Jesus, the leader and perfecter of faith. For the sake of the joy that lay before him he endured the cross, despising its shame, and has taken his seat at the right of the throne of God.

text.NRSV: Consider him who endured such hostility against himself from sinners, so that you may not grow weary or lose heart.
text.Rotherham: For take ye into consideration—him who hath endured, such contradiction, by sinners against themselves, lest ye be wearied, in your souls becoming exhausted.
text.DRB: For think diligently upon him that endured such opposition from sinners against himself; that you be not wearied, fainting in your minds.
text.KJV: For consider him that indured such contradiction of sinners against himselfe, lest ye be wearied and faint in your mindes.
text.NABRE: Consider how he endured such opposition from sinners, in order that you may not grow weary and lose heart.

text.NRSV: In your struggle against sin you have not yet resisted to the point of shedding your blood.
text.Rotherham: Not yet unto blood, have ye resisted, against sin, waging a contest;
text.DRB: For you have not yet resisted unto blood, striving against sin:
text.KJV: Yee haue not yet resisted vnto blood, striuing against sinne.
text.NABRE: In your struggle against sin you have not yet resisted to the point of shedding blood.

text.NRSV: And you have forgotten the exhortation that addresses you as children—“My child, do not regard lightly the discipline of the Lord, or lose heart when you are punished by him;
text.Rotherham: And ye have quite forgotten the exhortation which, indeed, with you as with sons, doth reason:—My son! be not slighting the discipline of the Lord, neither be fainting, when by him, thou art reproved;
text.DRB: And you have forgotten the consolation, which speaketh to you, as unto children, saying: My son, neglect not the discipline of the Lord; neither be thou wearied whilst thou art rebuked by him.
text.KJV: And ye haue forgotten the exhortation which speaketh vnto you as vnto children, My sonne, despise not thou the chastening of the Lord, nor faint when thou art rebuked of him.
text.NABRE: You have also forgotten the exhortation addressed to you as sons: “My son, do not disdain the discipline of the Lord or lose heart when reproved by him;

text.NRSV: for the Lord disciplines those whom he loves, and chastises every child whom he accepts.”
text.Rotherham: For, whom the Lord loveth, he doth, discipline, and scourgeth every son whom he doth welcome home.
text.DRB: For whom the Lord loveth, he chastiseth; and he scourgeth every son whom he receiveth.
text.KJV: For whome the Lord loueth hee chasteneth, and scourgeth euery sonne whom he receiueth.
text.NABRE: for whom the Lord loves, he disciplines; he scourges every son he acknowledges.”

text.NRSV: Endure trials for the sake of discipline. God is treating you as children; for what child is there whom a parent does not discipline?
text.Rotherham: For the sake of discipline, persevere! As towards sons, God, beareth himself, towards you; for who is a son whom a father doth not discipline?
text.DRB: Persevere under discipline. God dealeth with you as with his sons; for what son is there, whom the father doth not correct?
text.KJV: If yee endure chastening, God dealeth with you as with sonnes: for what sonne is he whom the father chasteneth not?
text.NABRE: Endure your trials as “discipline”; God treats you as sons. For what “son” is there whom his father does not discipline?

text.NRSV: If you do not have that discipline in which all children share, then you are illegitimate and not his children.
text.Rotherham: If however ye are without discipline, whereof, all, have received a share, then, are ye, bastards, and, not sons.
text.DRB: But if you be without chastisement, whereof all are made partakers, then are you bastards, and not sons.
text.KJV: But if ye be without chastisement, whereof all are partakers, then are ye bastards, and not sonnes.
text.NABRE: If you are without discipline, in which all have shared, you are not sons but bastards.

text.NRSV: Moreover, we had human parents to discipline us, and we respected them. Should we not be even more willing to be subject to the Father of spirits and live?
text.Rotherham: Furthermore, indeed, the fathers of our flesh, we used to have, as administrators of discipline, and we used to pay deference: shall we not, much rather, submit ourselves to the Father of our spirits and, live?
text.DRB: Moreover we have had fathers of our flesh, for instructors, and we reverenced them: shall we not much more obey the Father of spirits, and live?
text.KJV: Furthermore, wee haue had fathers of our flesh, which corrected vs, and we gaue them reuerence: shall we not much rather bee in subiection vnto the Father of Spirits, and liue?
text.NABRE: Besides this, we have had our earthly fathers to discipline us, and we respected them. Should we not [then] submit all the more to the Father of spirits and live?

text.NRSV: For they disciplined us for a short time as seemed best to them, but he disciplines us for our good, in order that we may share his holiness.
text.Rotherham: For, they, indeed, for a few days, according to that which seemed good to them, were administering discipline; but, he, unto that which is profitable, with view to our partaking of his holiness:
text.DRB: And they indeed for a few days, according to their own pleasure, instructed us: but he, for our profit, that we might receive his sanctification.
text.KJV: For they verily for a fewe dayes chastened vs after their owne pleasure, but hee for our profit, that we might bee partakers of his holinesse.
text.NABRE: They disciplined us for a short time as seemed right to them, but he does so for our benefit, in order that we may share his holiness.

text.NRSV: Now, discipline always seems painful rather than pleasant at the time, but later it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness to those who have been trained by it.
text.Rotherham: But, no discipline, for the present, indeed, seemeth to be of joy, but of sorrow: afterwards, however—to them who thereby have been trained, it yieldeth peaceful fruit, of righteousness.
text.DRB: Now all chastisement for the present indeed seemeth not to bring with it joy, but sorrow: but afterwards it will yield, to them that are exercised by it, the most peaceable fruit of justice.
text.KJV: Now no chastening for the present seemeth to be ioyous, but grieuous: neuerthelesse, afterward it yeeldeth the peaceable fruite of righteousnesse, vnto them which are exercised thereby.
text.NABRE: At the time, all discipline seems a cause not for joy but for pain, yet later it brings the peaceful fruit of righteousness to those who are trained by it.

text.NRSV: Therefore lift your drooping hands and strengthen your weak knees,
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, the slackened hands and paralysed knees, restore ye,
text.DRB: Wherefore lift up the hands which hang down, and the feeble knees,
text.KJV: Wherefore lift vp the handes which hang downe, and the feeble knees.
text.NABRE: So strengthen your drooping hands and your weak knees.

text.NRSV: and make straight paths for your feet, so that what is lame may not be put out of joint, but rather be healed.
text.Rotherham: And, straight tracks, be making for your feet—that the lame member may not be dislocated, but, be healed rather.
text.DRB: And make straight steps with your feet: that no one, halting, may go out of the way; but rather be healed.
text.KJV: And make straight paths for your feete, lest that which is lame bee turned out of the way, but let it rather bee healed.
text.NABRE: Make straight paths for your feet, that what is lame may not be dislocated but healed.

text.NRSV: Pursue peace with everyone, and the holiness without which no one will see the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Peace, be pursuing, with all, and the obtaining of holiness,—without which no one shall see the Lord:
text.DRB: Follow peace with all men, and holiness: without which no man shall see God.
text.KJV: Followe peace with all men, and holinesse, without which no man shall see the Lord:
text.NABRE: Penalties of Disobedience. Strive for peace with everyone, and for that holiness without which no one will see the Lord.

text.NRSV: See to it that no one fails to obtain the grace of God; that no “root of bitterness” springs up and causes trouble, and through it many become defiled.
text.Rotherham: Using oversight—lest any one be falling behind from the favour of God,—lest any root of bitterness, springing up above, be causing trouble, and, through it, the many, be defiled:
text.DRB: Looking diligently, lest any man be wanting to the grace of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up do hinder, and by it many be defiled.
text.KJV: Looking diligently, lest any man faile of the grace of God, lest any roote of bitternesse springing vp, trouble you, and thereby many be defiled:
text.NABRE: See to it that no one be deprived of the grace of God, that no bitter root spring up and cause trouble, through which many may become defiled,

text.NRSV: See to it that no one is immoral or godless like Esau, who sold his birthright for a single meal.
text.Rotherham: Lest there be any fornicator, or profane person, like Esau,—who, for the sake of one meal, yielded up his own firstborn rights;
text.DRB: Lest there be any fornicator, or profane person, as Esau; who for one mess, sold his first birthright.
text.KJV: Lest there bee any fornicatour, or profane person, as Esau, who for one morsell of meat sold his birthright.
text.NABRE: that no one be an immoral or profane person like Esau, who sold his birthright for a single meal.

text.NRSV: You know that later, when he wanted to inherit the blessing, he was rejected, for he found no chance to repent, even though he sought the blessing with tears.
text.Rotherham: For ye know that, afterwards—when he even wished to inherit the blessing, he was rejected; for, place of repentance, found he none, even though, with tears, he diligently sought it.
text.DRB: For know ye that afterwards, when he desired to inherit the benediction, he was rejected; for he found no place of repentance, although with tears he had sought it.
text.KJV: For yee know how that afterward when hee would haue inherited the blessing, hee was reiected: for hee found no place of repentance, though he sought it carefully with teares.
text.NABRE: For you know that later, when he wanted to inherit his father’s blessing, he was rejected because he found no opportunity to change his mind, even though he sought the blessing with tears.

text.NRSV: You have not come to something that can be touched, a blazing fire, and darkness, and gloom, and a tempest,
text.Rotherham: For ye have not approached—unto, a searching and scorching fire, and gloom, and mist, and tempest,
text.DRB: For you are not come to a mountain that might be touched, and a burning fire, and a whirlwind, and darkness, and storm,
text.KJV: For yee are not come vnto the mount that might be touched, and that burned with fire, nor vnto blacknesse, and darknes, and tempest,
text.NABRE: You have not approached that which could be touched and a blazing fire and gloomy darkness and storm

text.NRSV: and the sound of a trumpet, and a voice whose words made the hearers beg that not another word be spoken to them.
text.Rotherham: And a trumpets peal,—and a sound of things spoken:—from which they who heard excused themselves, lest there should be added to them, a word;
text.DRB: And the sound of a trumpet, and the voice of words, which they that heard excused themselves, that the word might not be spoken to them:
text.KJV: And the sound of a trumpet, and the voyce of wordes, which voyce they that heard, entreated that the word should not bee spoken to them any more.
text.NABRE: and a trumpet blast and a voice speaking words such that those who heard begged that no message be further addressed to them,

text.NRSV: (For they could not endure the order that was given, “If even an animal touches the mountain, it shall be stoned to death.”
text.Rotherham: For they could not bear, that which was being enjoined,—and, should a beast be touching the mountain, it shall be stoned;
text.DRB: For they did not endure that which was said: And if so much as a beast shall touch the mount, it shall be stoned.
text.KJV: For they could not indure that which was commaunded: And if so much as a beast touch the Mountaine, it shall be stoned, or thrust thorow with a dart.
text.NABRE: for they could not bear to hear the command: “If even an animal touches the mountain, it shall be stoned.”

text.NRSV: Indeed, so terrifying was the sight that Moses said, “I tremble with fear.”)
text.Rotherham: And, so fearful was that which was showing itself, Moses, said—I am terrified, and do tremble!
text.DRB: And so terrible was that which was seen, Moses said: I am frighted, and tremble.
text.KJV: And so terrible was the sight, that Moses sayde, I exceedingly feare, and quake.
text.NABRE: Indeed, so fearful was the spectacle that Moses said, “I am terrified and trembling.”

text.NRSV: But you have come to Mount Zion and to the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to innumerable angels in festal gathering,
text.Rotherham: But ye have approached—unto Zion’s mountain, and unto the city of a Living God, a heavenly Jerusalem,—and unto myriads of messengers,
text.DRB: But you are come to mount Sion, and to the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to the company of many thousands of angels,
text.KJV: But ye are come vnto mount Sion, and vnto the citie of the liuing God the heauenly Ierusalem, and to an innumerable company of Angels:
text.NABRE: No, you have approached Mount Zion and the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and countless angels in festal gathering,

text.NRSV: and to the assembly of the firstborn who are enrolled in heaven, and to God the judge of all, and to the spirits of the righteous made perfect,
text.Rotherham: in high festival,—and unto an assembly of firstborn ones, enrolled in the heavens,—and unto God, judge of all,—and unto the spirits of righteous ones made perfect,—
text.DRB: And to the church of the firstborn, who are written in the heavens, and to God the judge of all, and to the spirits of the just made perfect,
text.KJV: To the generall assembly, and Church of the first borne which are written in heauen, and to God the Iudge of all, and to the spirits of iust men made perfect:
text.NABRE: and the assembly of the firstborn enrolled in heaven, and God the judge of all, and the spirits of the just made perfect,

text.NRSV: and to Jesus, the mediator of a new covenant, and to the sprinkled blood that speaks a better word than the blood of Abel.
text.Rotherham: And unto the mediator of a new covenant, Jesus,—and unto the blood of sprinkling, more excellently speaking, than, Abel.
text.DRB: And to Jesus the mediator of the new testament, and to the sprinkling of blood which speaketh better than that of Abel.
text.KJV: And to Iesus the mediatour of the new Couenant, and to the blood of sprinckling, that speaketh better things then that of Abel.
text.NABRE: and Jesus, the mediator of a new covenant, and the sprinkled blood that speaks more eloquently than that of Abel.

text.NRSV: See that you do not refuse the one who is speaking; for if they did not escape when they refused the one who warned them on earth, how much less will we escape if we reject the one who warns from heaven!
text.Rotherham: Beware, lest ye excuse yourselves from him that speaketh; for, if, they escaped not, who excused themselves from him who on earth was warning, how much less, shall, we, who from him that warneth from the heavens, do turn ourselves away:
text.DRB: See that you refuse him not that speaketh. For if they escaped not who refused him that spoke upon the earth, much more shall not we, that turn away from him that speaketh to us from heaven.
text.KJV: See that yee refuse not him that speaketh: for if they escaped not who refused him that spake on earth, much more shall not we escape if wee turne away from him that speaketh from heauen.
text.NABRE: See that you do not reject the one who speaks. For if they did not escape when they refused the one who warned them on earth, how much more in our case if we turn away from the one who warns from heaven.

text.NRSV: At that time his voice shook the earth; but now he has promised, “Yet once more I will shake not only the earth but also the heaven.”
text.Rotherham: Whose voice shook the earth, then, but, now, hath he promised, saying—Yet once for all, I, will shake—not only the earth, but, also the heaven.
text.DRB: Whose voice then moved the earth; but now he promiseth, saying: Yet once more, and I will move not only the earth, but heaven also.
text.KJV: Whose voice then shooke the earth, but now he hath promised, saying, Yet once more I shake not the earth onely, but also heauen.
text.NABRE: His voice shook the earth at that time, but now he has promised, “I will once more shake not only earth but heaven.”

text.NRSV: This phrase, “Yet once more,” indicates the removal of what is shaken—that is, created things—so that what cannot be shaken may remain.
text.Rotherham: But, the saying, Yet once for all, maketh clear the removal of the things which can be shaken, as of things done with,—that they may remain, which cannot be shaken.
text.DRB: And in that he saith, Yet once more, he signifieth the translation of the moveable things as made, that those things may remain which are immoveable.
text.KJV: And this word Yet once more, signifieth the remouing of those things that are shaken, as of things that are made, that those things which cannot be shaken may remaine.
text.NABRE: That phrase, “once more,” points to [the] removal of shaken, created things, so that what is unshaken may remain.

text.NRSV: Therefore, since we are receiving a kingdom that cannot be shaken, let us give thanks, by which we offer to God an acceptable worship with reverence and awe;
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, seeing that, of a kingdom not to be shaken, we are receiving possession, let us have gratitude—whereby we may be rendering divine service well-pleasingly unto God, with reverence and awe;
text.DRB: Therefore receiving an immoveable kingdom, we have grace; whereby let us serve, pleasing God, with fear and reverence.
text.KJV: Wherefore wee receiuing a kingdome which cannot bee moued, let vs haue grace, whereby wee may serue God acceptably, with reuerence and godly feare.
text.NABRE: Therefore, we who are receiving the unshakable kingdom should have gratitude, with which we should offer worship pleasing to God in reverence and awe.

text.NRSV: for indeed our God is a consuming fire.
text.Rotherham: For, even our God, is a consuming fire.
text.DRB: For our God is a consuming fire.
text.KJV: For our God is a consuming fire.
text.NABRE: For our God is a consuming fire.

text.NRSV: Let mutual love continue.
text.Rotherham: Let brotherly love continue.
text.DRB: Let the charity of the brotherhood abide in you.
text.KJV: Let brotherly loue continue.
text.NABRE: VI. Final Exhortation, Blessing, Greetings Chapter 13 Let mutual love continue.

text.NRSV: Do not neglect to show hospitality to strangers, for by doing that some have entertained angels without knowing it.
text.Rotherham: Of the entertaining of strangers, be not forgetful, for, hereby, unawares, have some entertained, messengers.
text.DRB: And hospitality do not forget; for by this some, being not aware of it, have entertained angels.
text.KJV: Bee not forgetfull to entertaine strangers, for thereby some haue entertayned Angels vnawares.
text.NABRE: Do not neglect hospitality, for through it some have unknowingly entertained angels.

text.NRSV: Remember those who are in prison, as though you were in prison with them; those who are being tortured, as though you yourselves were being tortured.
text.Rotherham: Bear in mind them who are in bonds, as having become jointly bound,—them who are suffering ill-treatment, as being yourselves also in the body.
text.DRB: Remember them that are in bands, as if you were bound with them; and them that labour, as being yourselves also in the body.
text.KJV: Remember them that are in bonds, as bound with them; and them which suffer aduersitie, as being your selues also in the body.
text.NABRE: Be mindful of prisoners as if sharing their imprisonment, and of the ill-treated as of yourselves, for you also are in the body.

text.NRSV: Let marriage be held in honor by all, and let the marriage bed be kept undefiled; for God will judge fornicators and adulterers.
text.Rotherham: Let marriage be honourable in all, and the bed undefiled, for, fornicators and adulterers, God, will judge.
text.DRB: Marriage honourable in all , and the bed undefiled. For fornicators and adulterers God will judge.
text.KJV: Mariage is honorable in all, and the bed vndefiled: but whoremongers, and adulterers God will iudge.
text.NABRE: Let marriage be honored among all and the marriage bed be kept undefiled, for God will judge the immoral and adulterers.

text.NRSV: Keep your lives free from the love of money, and be content with what you have; for he has said, “I will never leave you or forsake you.”
text.Rotherham: Without fondness for money, be your way of life,—being content with the present things,—for, he, hath said: In nowise, thee, will I leave, no indeed! in nowise, thee, will I forsake:
text.DRB: Let your manners be without covetousness, contented with such things as you have; for he hath said: I will not leave thee, neither will I forsake thee.
text.KJV: Let your conuersation bee without couetousnesse: and be content with such things as yee haue. For hee hath said, I will neuer leaue thee, nor forsake thee.
text.NABRE: Let your life be free from love of money but be content with what you have, for he has said, “I will never forsake you or abandon you.”

text.NRSV: So we can say with confidence, “The Lord is my helper; I will not be afraid. What can anyone do to me?”
text.Rotherham: So that, taking courage, we may be saying—The Lord, hasteth to my cry,—I will not be put in fear: what shall, man, do unto me?
text.DRB: So that we may confidently say: The Lord is my helper: I will not fear what man shall do to me.
text.KJV: So that wee may boldly say, The Lord is my helper, and I will not feare what man shall doe vnto me.
text.NABRE: Thus we may say with confidence: “The Lord is my helper, [and] I will not be afraid. What can anyone do to me?”

text.NRSV: Remember your leaders, those who spoke the word of God to you; consider the outcome of their way of life, and imitate their faith.
text.Rotherham: Be mindful of them who are guiding you, who, indeed, have spoken unto you, the word of God: the outgoing of whose behaviour, reviewing, be imitating, their faith.
text.DRB: Remember your prelates who have spoken the word of God to you; whose faith follow, considering the end of their conversation,
text.KJV: Remember them which haue the rule ouer you, who haue spoken vnto you the word of God, whose faith follow, considering the end of their conuersation.
text.NABRE: Remember your leaders who spoke the word of God to you. Consider the outcome of their way of life and imitate their faith.

text.NRSV: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever.
text.Rotherham: Jesus Christ, yesterday, and to-day, is the same,—and unto the ages.
text.DRB: Jesus Christ, yesterday, and today; and the same for ever.
text.KJV: Iesus Christ the same yesterday, and to day, and for euer.
text.NABRE: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.

text.NRSV: Do not be carried away by all kinds of strange teachings; for it is well for the heart to be strengthened by grace, not by regulations about food, which have not benefited those who observe them.
text.Rotherham: With teachings, manifold and strange, be not carried aside; for it is, noble, that, with gratitude, should, the heart, be getting confirmed,—not with matters of food, in which, they who are walking, have not been profited.
text.DRB: Be not led away with various and strange doctrines. For it is best that the heart be established with grace, not with meats; which have not profited those that walk in them.
text.KJV: Be not caried about with diuers and strange doctrines: for it is a good thing that the heart be established with grace, not with meates, which haue not profited them that haue beene occupied therein.
text.NABRE: Do not be carried away by all kinds of strange teaching. It is good to have our hearts strengthened by grace and not by foods, which do not benefit those who live by them.

text.NRSV: We have an altar from which those who officiate in the tent have no right to eat.
text.Rotherham: We have an altar—to eat out of which, they, have no right, who in the tent are doing divine service;
text.DRB: We have an altar, whereof they have no power to eat who serve the tabernacle.
text.KJV: Wee haue an altar whereof they haue no right to eate, which serue the Tabernacle.
text.NABRE: We have an altar from which those who serve the tabernacle have no right to eat.

text.NRSV: For the bodies of those animals whose blood is brought into the sanctuary by the high priest as a sacrifice for sin are burned outside the camp.
text.Rotherham: For, in the case of those living creatures, whose blood for sins is carried into the holy place, through means of the high-priest, the bodies of these, are burned up, outside the camp.
text.DRB: For the bodies of those beasts, whose blood is brought into the holies by the high priest for sin, are burned without the camp.
text.KJV: For the bodies of those beasts, whose blood is brought into the Sanctuary by the high Priest for sinne, are burnt without the campe.
text.NABRE: The bodies of the animals whose blood the high priest brings into the sanctuary as a sin offering are burned outside the camp.

text.NRSV: Therefore Jesus also suffered outside the city gate in order to sanctify the people by his own blood.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, Jesus also, that he might hallow the people through means of his own blood, outside the gate, suffered:
text.DRB: Wherefore Jesus also, that he might sanctify the people by his own blood, suffered without the gate.
text.KJV: Wherefore Iesus also, that hee might sanctifie the people with his own blood, suffered without the gate.
text.NABRE: Therefore, Jesus also suffered outside the gate, to consecrate the people by his own blood.

text.NRSV: Let us then go to him outside the camp and bear the abuse he endured.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, let us be going forth unto him, outside the camp, his reproach bearing.
text.DRB: Let us go forth therefore to him without the camp, bearing his reproach .
text.KJV: Let vs goe foorth therefore vnto him without the campe, bearing his reproch.
text.NABRE: Let us then go to him outside the camp, bearing the reproach that he bore.

text.NRSV: For here we have no lasting city, but we are looking for the city that is to come.
text.Rotherham: For we have not here an abiding city, but, unto that which is to be, are we seeking our way.
text.DRB: For we have not here a lasting city, but we seek one that is to come.
text.KJV: For here haue we no continuing citie, but we seeke one to come.
text.NABRE: For here we have no lasting city, but we seek the one that is to come.

text.NRSV: Through him, then, let us continually offer a sacrifice of praise to God, that is, the fruit of lips that confess his name.
text.Rotherham: Through him, let us be offering up a sacrifice of praise, continually, unto God; that is, a fruit of lips confessing unto his name.
text.DRB: By him therefore let us offer the sacrifice of praise always to God, that is to say, the fruit of lips confessing to his name.
text.KJV: By him therefore let vs offer the sacrifice of praise to God continually, that is, the fruit of our lippes, giuing thankes to his Name.
text.NABRE: Through him [then] let us continually offer God a sacrifice of praise, that is, the fruit of lips that confess his name.

text.NRSV: Do not neglect to do good and to share what you have, for such sacrifices are pleasing to God.
text.Rotherham: But, of the doing good and of fellowship, be not forgetful; for, with such sacrifices as these, is, God, well-pleased.
text.DRB: And do not forget to do good, and to impart; for by such sacrifices God's favour is obtained.
text.KJV: But to doe good, and to communicate forget not, for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.
text.NABRE: Do not neglect to do good and to share what you have; God is pleased by sacrifices of that kind.

text.NRSV: Obey your leaders and submit to them, for they are keeping watch over your souls and will give an account. Let them do this with joy and not with sighing—for that would be harmful to you.
text.Rotherham: Be yielding unto them who are guiding you, and submit yourselves; for they are watching over your souls, as having an account to render, that, with joy the same they may be doing, and not with sighing,—for, unprofitable unto you, were, this.
text.DRB: Obey your prelates, and be subject to them. For they watch as being to render an account of your souls; that they may do this with joy, and not with grief. For this is not expedient for you.
text.KJV: Obey them that haue the rule ouer you, and submit your selues: for they watch for your soules, as they that must giue account, that they may doe it with ioy, and not with griefe: for that is vnprofitable for you.
text.NABRE: Obey your leaders and defer to them, for they keep watch over you and will have to give an account, that they may fulfill their task with joy and not with sorrow, for that would be of no advantage to you.

text.NRSV: Pray for us; for we are sure that we have a clear conscience, desiring to act honorably in all things.
text.Rotherham: Be praying for us; for we persuade ourselves that an honourable conscience have we, in all things honourably, desiring to behave ourselves.
text.DRB: Pray for us. For we trust we have a good conscience, being willing to behave ourselves well in all things.
text.KJV: Pray for vs: for we trust wee haue a good conscience in all things, willing to liue honestly.
text.NABRE: Pray for us, for we are confident that we have a clear conscience, wishing to act rightly in every respect.

text.NRSV: I urge you all the more to do this, so that I may be restored to you very soon.
text.Rotherham: But, much more abundantly, do I exhort you the same to do, that, more speedily, I may be restored, unto you.
text.DRB: And I beseech you the more to do this, that I may be restored to you the sooner.
text.KJV: But I beseech you the rather to doe this, that I may be restored to you the sooner.
text.NABRE: I especially ask for your prayers that I may be restored to you very soon.

text.NRSV: Now may the God of peace, who brought back from the dead our Lord Jesus, the great shepherd of the sheep, by the blood of the eternal covenant,
text.Rotherham: But, the God of peace, He that led up from among the dead the great Shepherd of the sheep, with the blood of an age-abiding covenant,—our Lord Jesus,
text.DRB: And may the God of peace, who brought again from the dead the great pastor of the sheep, our Lord Jesus Christ, in the blood of the everlasting testament,
text.KJV: Now the God of peace, that brought againe from the dead our Lord Iesus, that great shepheard of the sheepe, through the blood of the euerlasting Couenant,
text.NABRE: May the God of peace, who brought up from the dead the great shepherd of the sheep by the blood of the eternal covenant, Jesus our Lord,

text.NRSV: make you complete in everything good so that you may do his will, working among us that which is pleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
text.Rotherham: Fit you, by every good work, for the doing of his will, doing within us, that which is well-pleasing, before him through Jesus Christ: to whom be the glory, unto the ages of ages. Amen.
text.DRB: Fit you in all goodness, that you may do his will; doing in you that which is well pleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ, to whom is glory for ever and ever. Amen.
text.KJV: Make you perfect in euery good worke to doe his will, working in you that which is well pleasing in his sight, through Iesus Christ, to whom be glorie for euer and euer. Amen.
text.NABRE: furnish you with all that is good, that you may do his will. May he carry out in you what is pleasing to him through Jesus Christ, to whom be glory forever [and ever]. Amen.

text.NRSV: I appeal to you, brothers and sisters, bear with my word of exhortation, for I have written to you briefly.
text.Rotherham: Now I exhort you, brethren, bear with the word of exhortation,—for, even with brief [words], have I sent unto you.
text.DRB: And I beseech you, brethren, that you suffer this word of consolation. For I have written to you in a few words.
text.KJV: And I beseech you brethren, suffer the word of exhortation, for I haue written a letter vnto you in few words.
text.NABRE: Brothers, I ask you to bear with this message of encouragement, for I have written to you rather briefly.

text.NRSV: I want you to know that our brother Timothy has been set free; and if he comes in time, he will be with me when I see you.
text.Rotherham: Know ye that our brother Timothy hath been set at liberty,—with whom, if more speedily he be coming, I will see you.
text.DRB: Know ye that our brother Timothy is set at liberty: with whom (if he come shortly) I will see you.
text.KJV: Know yee, that our brother Timothie is set at libertie, with whom if he come shortly, I will see you.
text.NABRE: I must let you know that our brother Timothy has been set free. If he comes soon, I shall see you together with him.

text.NRSV: Greet all your leaders and all the saints. Those from Italy send you greetings.
text.Rotherham: Salute all them who are guiding you, and all the saints: they from Italy, salute you.
text.DRB: Salute all your prelates, and all the saints. The brethren from Italy salute you.
text.KJV: Salute all them that haue the rule ouer you, and al the Saints. They of Italy salute you.
text.NABRE: Greetings to all your leaders and to all the holy ones. Those from Italy send you greetings.

text.NRSV: Grace be with all of you.
text.Rotherham: Favour be with you all.
text.DRB: Grace be with you all. Amen.
text.KJV: Grace be with you all. Amen.
text.NABRE: Grace be with all of you.

5.1: For every high priest taken from among men, is ordained for men in the things that appertain to God, that he may offer up gifts and sacrifices for sins:
5.2: Who can have compassion on them that are ignorant and that err: because he himself also is compassed with infirmity.
5.3: And therefore he ought, as for the people, so also for himself, to offer for sins.
5.4: Neither doth any man take the honour to himself, but he that is called by God, as Aaron was.
5.5: So Christ also did not glorify himself, that he might be made a high priest: but he that said unto him: Thou art my Son, this day have I begotten thee.
5.6: As he saith also in another place: Thou art a priest for ever, according to the order of Melchisedech.
5.7: Who in the days of his flesh, with a strong cry and tears, offering up prayers and supplications to him that was able to save him from death, was heard for his reverence.
5.8: And whereas indeed he was the Son of God, he learned obedience by the things which he suffered:
